commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=081f414e059e78b3b419f22be1706aea6ae4af69)
commands.review_summary:
Adds an early memory-core `before_agent_reply` guard that returns handled for cron-triggered legacy light/rem token messages.

PR surface: Source +11. Total +11 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? No; source inspection supports the stale legacy `agentTurn.message` token path, but no live run summary or after-fix cron run was inspected.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs author or maintainer changes plus after-fix real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not auto-repair while contributor setup proof is absent.

target.title: fix(memory-core): intercept legacy dreaming tokens in before_agent_reply hook
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Legacy dreaming cron tokens (`__openclaw_memory_core_light_sleep__` and `__openclaw_memory_core_rem_sleep__`) were not recognized by the `before_agent_reply` hook handler. When the cron job fired, the message containing the double-underscore-wrapped token reached the LLM. With `lightContext: true` stripping all bootstrap context files, the LLM had no guidance and attempted to execute the token as a shell command, producing `zsh: command not found` errors on every dreaming cycle.

The managed dreaming token (`__openclaw_memory_core_short_term_promotion_dream__`) was already properly intercepted, but the legacy tokens (light_sleep / rem_sleep) were missing from the guard.

## Why This Change Was Made

The `before_agent_reply` hook at `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts:929` already checks for the managed `DREAMING_SYSTEM_EVENT_TEXT` token and handles it via `runShortTermDreamingPromotionIfTriggered()`. The two legacy tokens are defined at `src/memory-host-sdk/dreaming.ts:26-29` as `LEGACY_MEMORY_LIGHT_DREAMING_EVENT_TEXT` and `LEGACY_MEMORY_REM_DREAMING_EVENT_TEXT`.

The fix adds a check for both legacy tokens before the managed token check, returning `{ handled: true }` early so the LLM is never invoked for these system events. The check is scoped to `ctx.trigger === "cron"` since these tokens are only expected from cron job payloads.

## Evidence

### Before (shell error on every cycle)
The legacy tokens reached the LLM, which tried to execute them as shell commands:
```
zsh:1: command not found: __openclaw_memory_core_light_sleep__
```

### After (token intercepted before LLM invocation)
With the fix, the `before_agent_reply` hook now recognizes legacy tokens and returns early:
- Legacy dreaming tokens (light_sleep / rem_sleep) no longer reach the LLM
- The managed dreaming token (short_term_promotion_dream) continues to work as before
- Non-cron triggers are unaffected (the guard requires `ctx.trigger === "cron"`)

### Test Results
```
✓ extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts     51 passed
✓ extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts  50 passed
```

## Merge Risk

Low. The change adds a single early-return guard before the existing managed token handling. It only affects cron-triggered events containing the specific legacy token strings. The managed dreaming path and all non-cron paths are unchanged.
